subject: Joint Distributions
topic: §42 Sum of Two Independent Random Variables: Continuous Case
question: The joint distribution function of $X$ and $Y$ is given by $f_{XY}(x, y) = \begin{cases} \frac{3}{11}(5x + y) & x, y > 0, x + 2y < 2 \\ 0 & \text{elsewhere} \end{cases}$ Find the probability density of $Z = X + Y$.
explanation: Note first that the region of integration is the interior of the triangle with vertices at $(0, 0), (0, 1)$, and $(2, 0)$. From Figure 42.2, we see that $F(a) = 0$ if $a < 0$. If $0 \le a < 1$ then $F_Z(a) = \Pr(Z \le a) = \int_0^a \int_0^{a-y} \frac{3}{11}(5x + y)\,dx\,dy = \frac{3}{11}a^3$. If $1 \le a < 2$ then the two lines $x + y = a$ and $x + 2y = 2$ intersect at $(2a - 2, 2 - a)$. In this case, $F_Z(a) = \Pr(Z \le a) = \int_0^{2-a} \int_0^{a-y} \frac{3}{11}(5x + y)\,dx\,dy + \int_{2-a}^1 \int_0^{2-2y} \frac{3}{11}(5x + y)\,dx\,dy = \frac{3}{11}\left(-\frac{7}{3}a^3 + 9a^2 - 8a + 7\right)$. If $a \ge 2$ then $F_Z(a)$ is the area of the shaded triangle which is equal to 1. Differentiating with respect to $a$ we find $f_Z(a) = \begin{cases} \frac{9}{11}a^2 & 0 < a \le 1 \\ \frac{3}{11}(-7a^2 + 18a - 8) & 1 < a < 2 \\ 0 & \text{elsewhere} \end{cases}$ Figure 42.2
